perturbation.original_text: The term “Representatives” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Representatives” shall include only the officers, and attorneys of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable. No other parties will be consider representatives for legal binding.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Representatives' in Section 2(b) is narrowed to only include officers and attorneys. This directly contradicts the broader definition provided in the original text, which includes directors, employees, agents, partners, advisors, accountants, consultants, and bankers. This creates ambiguity because certain actions taken by individuals who would be 'Representatives' under the original definition but not under the modified definition might not be subject to the same obligations or restrictions under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s 1 Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Representatives' to only officers and attorneys, explicitly excluding other parties. The contradicted text states that the Recipient shall cause its Representatives to use the Evaluation Material solely for evaluating a Possible Transaction. Since the definition of Representatives has been narrowed, those previously considered Representatives (e.g., employees, agents, partners) are no longer bound by the same obligations in the contradicted text. This creates a contradiction because the Recipient is now only responsible for the breach of the agreement by officers and attorneys, not the broader group originally intended. This is a strong in-text contradiction, as the modified definition directly conflicts with the obligations outlined in the contradicted text, making enforcement unclear and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s 1 Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient MAY, and it MAY cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s 1 Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall" to "may" in Section 3, the obligation for the Recipient to use Evaluation Material solely for the stated purpose and to keep it confidential is weakened. This creates a contradiction if Section 10, which states 'Each Recipient agrees not to use any Evaluation Material of the Provider in violation of applicable securities laws,' still assumes a strict obligation for confidentiality and appropriate use. Now it will not be a violation if the Evaluation material is not used solely for the the possible transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient agrees not to use any Evaluation Material of the Provider in violation of applicable securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall', creating a strict obligation to use the Evaluation Material solely for evaluating a Possible Transaction and to keep it confidential. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may', weakening this obligation. The Contradicted Text states, 'Each Recipient agrees not to use any Evaluation Material of the Provider in violation of applicable securities laws.' Because the 'shall' was changed to 'may', the obligation to keep the Evaluation Material confidential is weakened, therefore it could be considered that it will no longer be a violation of applicable securities laws if the material is not used soley for the transaction. This creates an in-text contradiction as the change weakens the obligation and enforcement of the original text, leading to uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to, within five business days of receipt of such notice, destroy or return all Evaluation Material, except for that portion of notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which does not contain in any respect any of the Evaluation Materials, in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained, except that Recipient’s outside legal counsel may retain one copy to be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of establishing Recipient’s compliance with its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to, within thirty business days of receipt of such notice, destroy or return all Evaluation Material, except for that portion of notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which does not contain in any respect any of the Evaluation Materials, in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained, except that Recipient’s outside legal counsel may retain one copy to be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of establishing Recipient’s compliance with its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'five business days' to 'thirty business days' in Section 6 creates a direct contradiction with the original requirement for prompt action. If Section 14 states 'money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement,' delaying the return or destruction of evaluation material for an additional 25 days introduces a significant risk that confidential information could be used inappropriately, making the remedy insufficient. It also is a contradiction of time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives and that the Company against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Company of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'five business days' to 'thirty business days' directly contradicts the provision in the contradicted text stating that 'money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach'. The explanation clearly states that this delay introduces a risk of inappropriate use of confidential information, rendering the remedy insufficient. This directly violates the intent of the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the provided scenario.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Recipient will, within one year from the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly solicit the employment or consulting services of or employ or engage as a consultant any of the officers or employees of the Provider, so long as they are employed by the Provider and for three months after they cease to be employed by Provider. A Recipient is not prohibited from soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at (i) any particular individual or (ii) the employees of the Provider generally.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Recipient will, within six months from the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly solicit the employment or consulting services of or employ or engage as a consultant any of the officers or employees of the Provider, so long as they are employed by the Provider and for three months after they cease to be employed by Provider. A Recipient is not prohibited from soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at (i) any particular individual or (ii) the employees of the Provider generally.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the non-solicitation period from one year to six months in Section 7 creates a potential conflict with the general purpose of the agreement, which is to protect sensitive information during the evaluation period. If the evaluation period is ongoing and potentially extends beyond six months, this shortened restriction could allow premature solicitation of employees who possess valuable insights into the Provider's confidential information. The overall agreement says that the term should be two years. This shows the In-Text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate two years after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a non-solicitation period of one year, whereas the changed text reduces this to six months. The contradicted text states that the agreement terminates two years after the date of the agreement. The perturbation explanation highlights that this change introduces a contradiction because the agreement is supposed to protect sensitive information during the evaluation period, and shortening the non-solicitation period creates a conflict with the overall agreement's term of two years. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as the key obligations have been defined inconsistently, creating difficulty in enforcement and unclear interpretation.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by the other Party, neither it nor any of its affiliates (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) or Representatives (acting in any capacity other than as an advisor in any of the following cases) will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Exchange Act) with respect to the securities of the other Party; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit an unsolicited proposal for or offer of (with or without condition), any extraordinary transaction involving the other Party or its securities or assets; (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other Party; (e) take any action which might force the other Party to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (f) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by the other Party, neither it nor any of its affiliates (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) or Representatives (acting in any capacity other than as an advisor in any of the following cases) will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the other Party; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Exchange Act) with respect to the securities of the other Party; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit an unsolicited proposal for or offer of (with or without condition), any extraordinary transaction involving the other Party or its securities or assets; (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other Party; (e) take any action which might force the other Party to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above.
perturbation.explanation: By removing point (f) from Section 8, which restricts parties from entering into discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to the other listed actions, a contradiction is created. The removal of the specific restriction against discussions with third parties directly conflicts with the general intent of the Standstill provision to prevent actions that could influence the management or policies of the other party, leading to a potential violation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other Party
perturbation.justification: The original text included clause (f), restricting parties from discussing arrangements with third parties regarding actions that could influence the other party's management or policies. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text states that parties cannot 'otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other Party'. By removing the restriction on discussions with third parties, the changed text now permits actions (discussions with third parties) that could lead to a violation of the contradicted text (actions seeking to control or influence management), creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition provided for 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Company understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Companies unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Company hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Companies shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Each Company also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Company will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the 4 matters specifically agreed to herein. Neither Company is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and either Company may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Company at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Company understands and agrees that a contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction EXISTS between the Companies without the need for a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction, and each Company does not waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction. Each Company also agrees that, legal obligations of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction arise by virtue of this Agreement even before a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, even for the 4 matters not specifically agreed to herein. Neither Company is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and either Company may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Company at any time.
perturbation.explanation: By altering Section 11 to state that a contract EXISTS without a final definitive agreement, a contradiction arises with the original intent. The original text explicitly states the opposite to avoid premature obligations. The contradiction introduces legal uncertainty, it makes it harder to enforce the agreement. The text also states that a company will not waive in advance any claims, however, since no legal binding has taken place, this text will be consider inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Companies regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements and. discussions between the Companies regarding such subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 directly contradicts the provided text concerning the entire agreement. The original text asserts that no contract exists without a final definitive agreement, and companies waive related claims. The modified text, however, states that a contract EXISTS without such an agreement and that companies do not waive claims. This fundamentally conflicts with the contradicted text, which states the agreement contains the "entire agreement" because it is clear that the terms are in direct opposition.
